content: Bevy supports rendering to a texture (camera render target = Handle<Image>) and since Bevy 0.15 a stable 'GPU Readback' feature reads rendered GPU frames back to CPU for screenshots/compositing (bevy 0.15 release notes verified 2026-06-26). Bevy is now at 0.19.0 (crates.io verified 2026-06-26); Scrysynth ships 0.18.1. This enables the offscreen-render->stream-frames-to-webview compositing path for visuals-behind-grid. Combined with Tauri 2 transparent windows (macOSPrivateApi:true + window transparent:true) this gives two viable compositing strategies: (A) transparent Bevy overlay window z-ordered behind a transparent main Tauri window, React Flow canvas translucent; (B) Bevy GPU readback streaming frames into a webview <canvas>. Strategy A is cheaper and matches Bespoke-Synth feel but Linux/Wayland window stacking is unreliable; Strategy B is true single-window but costs readback+IPC bandwidth.
